title: Evaluating Large Language Models Trained on Code
claim_supported: Codex/HumanEval framed early AI coding support as code synthesis from prompts and docstrings, with safety and limitation caveats.
limitations_or_bias: Preprint by model vendor; HumanEval is narrow and not production software engineering.
verification_status: Verified: URL, title, authors and date checked.

title: GitHub Copilot is generally available to all developers
claim_supported: GitHub Copilot GA was an editor-based assistant focused on suggestions, completions and pair-programming workflow.
limitations_or_bias: Vendor source; useful for product timeline, not independent productivity proof.
verification_status: Verified: URL, title, organisation and date checked.

title: GitHub Copilot X: The AI-powered developer experience
claim_supported: Copilot X marked expansion from autocomplete into chat, pull requests, documentation and CLI-oriented assistance.
limitations_or_bias: Vendor source; roadmap framing and capability claims need independent evaluation.
verification_status: Verified via search result and official GitHub domain.

title: SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-World GitHub Issues?
claim_supported: SWE-bench moved evaluation towards repository-level issue resolution over real GitHub issues and pull requests.
limitations_or_bias: Open-source benchmark with contamination and test-validity limitations; Python-heavy.
verification_status: Verified: URL, title, authors and date checked.

title: The Impact of AI on Developer Productivity: Evidence from GitHub Copilot
author_or_org: Sida Peng; Eirini Kalliamvakou; Peter Cihon; Mert Demirer
claim_supported: In a bounded JavaScript HTTP-server task, participants with Copilot completed faster than controls.
limitations_or_bias: Single lab task; speed was measured more directly than long-term maintainability or public-sector assurance.
verification_status: Verified: URL, title, authors and date checked.

title: Asleep at the Keyboard? Assessing the Security of GitHub Copilot's Code Contributions
claim_supported: AI code suggestions can include vulnerable code, so generated code must be security-reviewed.
relevant_quote_short: About 40 per cent vulnerable in study.
limitations_or_bias: Early Copilot-era study; prompt scenarios may not represent every modern model or workflow.
verification_status: Verified: URL, title, authors and date checked.

title: Do Users Write More Insecure Code with AI Assistants?
author_or_org: Neil Perry; Megha Srivastava; Deepak Kumar; Dan Boneh
claim_supported: Users with an AI code assistant wrote less secure code in security tasks and were more likely to believe it was secure.
limitations_or_bias: Security tasks and one Codex-era assistant; modern tools differ, but overconfidence risk remains relevant.
verification_status: Verified: URL, title, authors and date checked.

title: Introducing Devin, the first AI software engineer
claim_supported: Devin was presented as an autonomous software-engineering agent with shell, editor and browser in a sandbox.
limitations_or_bias: Vendor benchmark/performance claims are not independent evidence and must be labelled as claims.
verification_status: Verified: URL, title, author organisation and date checked.

title: Amazon Q Developer is now generally available
claim_supported: Amazon Q Developer GA included coding, testing, debugging, troubleshooting and transformation capabilities across the SDLC.
limitations_or_bias: Vendor source; not independent productivity or quality evidence.
verification_status: Verified: URL, title, organisation and date checked.

title: Claude 3.7 Sonnet and Claude Code
claim_supported: Claude Code entered limited research preview as an agentic command-line coding tool able to search, edit, test and use command-line tools.
limitations_or_bias: Vendor benchmark and productivity claims need independent support.
verification_status: Verified: URL, title, organisation and date checked.

title: Introducing Codex
claim_supported: OpenAI introduced Codex as a cloud-based software-engineering agent that can inspect a repo, edit files, run commands and propose PRs.
limitations_or_bias: Vendor source; use for release date, capability and stated controls, not independent efficacy.
verification_status: Verified: URL, title, organisation and date checked.

title: Codex changelog
claim_supported: The Codex product and CLI were actively changing through May 2026, including CLI releases and API/model updates.
limitations_or_bias: Changelog is product documentation, not independent evaluation.
verification_status: Verified: URL, title and current May 2026 entries checked.

title: Agent approvals and security
claim_supported: Codex uses sandbox and approval controls; these are important controls but not a substitute for human assurance.
limitations_or_bias: Vendor security design documentation; implementation details may change.

title: Build with Jules, your asynchronous coding agent
claim_supported: Google Jules public beta described an asynchronous coding agent using cloud VMs, repository context and human-visible diffs.
limitations_or_bias: Vendor source; capability and release date only.
verification_status: Verified: URL, title, organisation and date checked.

title: Jules, our asynchronous coding agent, is now available for everyone
claim_supported: Jules moved out of beta with tiered access and Gemini 2.5-powered asynchronous coding workflows.
limitations_or_bias: Vendor source; adoption counts are product claims.
verification_status: Verified via official Google result; date from result and article context.

title: GitHub Introduces Coding Agent For GitHub Copilot
claim_supported: GitHub introduced an asynchronous Copilot coding agent integrated with GitHub and Actions, with draft PRs and human approval controls.
limitations_or_bias: Vendor source; task-suitability claims need independent evaluation.
verification_status: Verified: URL, title, organisation and date checked.

title: About GitHub Copilot cloud agent
claim_supported: GitHub documentation describes assigning work to Copilot cloud agent, reviewing diffs and creating pull requests.
limitations_or_bias: Current docs can change; product availability varies by plan and policy.
verification_status: Verified via official GitHub Docs search result.

title: Claude Code overview
claim_supported: Claude Code is documented as an agentic coding tool available across terminal, IDE, desktop app and browser surfaces.
limitations_or_bias: Current docs can change; capability does not imply safe production use.

title: Measuring AI Ability to Complete Long Tasks
claim_supported: Long-task autonomy is better framed by task duration and reliability than by short benchmark success alone.
limitations_or_bias: METR methodology choices and extrapolation assumptions need caution.
verification_status: Verified: URL, title, organisation and date checked.

title: Measuring the Impact of Early-2025 AI on Experienced Open-Source Developer Productivity
claim_supported: In one realistic experienced-developer setting, early-2025 AI tools slowed task completion despite positive user expectations.
limitations_or_bias: Small sample and specific repositories/tools; not a universal negative result.
verification_status: Verified: URL, title, organisation and date checked.

title: Why SWE-bench Verified no longer measures frontier coding capabilities
claim_supported: OpenAI argued SWE-bench Verified had become unsuitable for frontier launch measurement because of flawed tests and contamination.
limitations_or_bias: Vendor analysis, but directly relevant to benchmark limitations and model-release incentives.
verification_status: Verified: URL, title, organisation and date checked.

title: State of AI-assisted Software Development 2025
claim_supported: DORA provides survey-oriented evidence on AI-assisted software development adoption and delivery effects.
limitations_or_bias: Survey evidence is self-reported and correlational; not causal procurement evidence.
verification_status: Verified: URL opens and title checked; detailed findings require report download/review.

title: AI Playbook for the UK Government
claim_supported: UK public-sector AI use should include lawful, secure, proportionate use, meaningful human control, lifecycle management and assurance.
limitations_or_bias: General AI guidance, not coding-agent-specific controls.
verification_status: Verified: URL, title, organisation and date checked.

title: Guidelines for secure AI system development
claim_supported: Secure AI systems need secure design, development, deployment, operation and maintenance across the lifecycle.
limitations_or_bias: Guidance addresses AI systems broadly; coding-agent delivery tooling needs local tailoring.

title: Create a secure service which protects users' privacy
claim_supported: Government services must be secure and protect privacy; prototypes need additional evidence before production claims.
limitations_or_bias: Service Standard applies to services, not all internal experiments; still relevant to production continuation.

title: Secure by Design Policy
claim_supported: Secure by Design requires accountable, risk-informed security activity throughout government digital delivery.
limitations_or_bias: Scope and mandatory status depend on organisation/service context.

title: Data protection by design and by default
claim_supported: Privacy and data-protection controls should be designed in from the start, not added after deployment.
limitations_or_bias: General UK GDPR guidance; specific DPIA/legal advice depends on use case.

title: Amazon Q Developer rename - Summary of changes
claim_supported: Amazon CodeWhisperer became part of Amazon Q Developer on 30 April 2024.
limitations_or_bias: Vendor documentation; capability and naming only.
verification_status: Verified via official AWS Docs search result.

title: Challenge 2 Dark Data Implementation
claim_supported: The repo is a worked Challenge 2 implementation with wiki, workbench, evaluation harness, MCP tooling and postmortem evidence.
limitations_or_bias: README is project narrative; corroborate with implementation files and reports.

title: Challenge 2: Unlocking the dark data
claim_supported: The problem is government guidance and policy material that exists but is hard to find, structure and use safely.
limitations_or_bias: Challenge brief is a problem statement, not evidence of prototype performance.

title: Repository Operating Rules
claim_supported: Repository rules require documentation lockstep, immutable source data, narrow validation and synthetic fixture handling.
limitations_or_bias: Rules express intended process; evidence of adherence comes from tracking docs and validation.

title: Challenge 2 LLM Wiki Operating Rules
claim_supported: Challenge 2 rules define raw sources as read-only and generated wiki claims as traceable to source notes and paths.
limitations_or_bias: Rules do not prove every generated claim is correct; they set the operating contract.

title: Context
claim_supported: Context records project purpose, architecture assumptions, data assumptions, important paths and evaluation/audit assumptions.
limitations_or_bias: Living document; must be read with code and reports.

title: Progress
claim_supported: Progress records completed work, current validation evidence, evaluation results and next steps.
limitations_or_bias: Long living document; may include historical entries.

title: Changelog
claim_supported: Changelog records meaningful repository changes and documentation lockstep entries.
limitations_or_bias: Useful chronology but may summarise rather than prove each behaviour.

title: Challenge 2 Knowledge Base Index
claim_supported: The generated wiki includes 43 sources across six formats, topic pages, entity pages, maps and source-register links.
limitations_or_bias: Generated artifact; correctness depends on builder and review.

title: Challenge 2 Architecture
claim_supported: The architecture keeps raw documents untouched and uses a repeatable builder to create source notes, topics, entities, maps, data and lint reports.
limitations_or_bias: Generated documentation; corroborated by builder and file tree.

title: Dark Data Workbench
claim_supported: The workbench supports question capture, search, facets, source cards, context sets, Browser AI export and MCP context export.
limitations_or_bias: Documentation of implementation; corroborate with workbench code/tests.

title: Challenge 2 Demonstration Guide
claim_supported: The demo route centres provenance as the product and shows source corpus, Obsidian validation, workbench, Browser AI export, benchmark and audit pack.
limitations_or_bias: Demo guide is a narrative artifact, not production evidence.

title: Challenge 2 Wiki Evaluation Benchmark
claim_supported: The repo contains a 100-question wiki-only benchmark with 0-5 rubrics, gold answers, source IDs and scoring caps.
limitations_or_bias: Benchmark is local and synthetic; does not measure production readiness.

title: Challenge 2 Wiki Evaluation Harness
claim_supported: The harness records prompts, answers, source access, audit cards, manifests and DSAP-style bundles for model comparison.
limitations_or_bias: Raw runs are external and ignored by Git; committed reports are summaries.

title: Challenge 2 Wiki Evaluation: MCP Comparison
claim_supported: Validated run summary reports 100/100 effective completions for Codex, Codex-MCP, Claude and Microsoft Copilot, with Gemini quota-limited and Copilot CLI policy-blocked.
limitations_or_bias: Local synthetic benchmark; scoring needs independent moderation for official comparative claims.

title: Challenge 2 Wiki Evaluation Rubric Leaderboard
claim_supported: The committed leaderboard records public-safe rubric scores without raw prompts or raw answer text.
limitations_or_bias: Not an official benchmark; scored against a local synthetic wiki benchmark.

title: Challenge 2 Realtime Delivery Report
claim_supported: The delivery report reconstructs the 2026-04-16 build sequence and validation from repo history, logs and postmortem evidence.
limitations_or_bias: Contains some private-source summary; use committed repo chronology for public claims.

title: Codex Contribution Modes and Government Security Assessment
claim_supported: The assessment concludes Codex was strong for Explorer, Builder, Refiner and Verifier, but not final authority for Security Steward or autonomous Operator work.
limitations_or_bias: Repo-local first-pass assessment, not formal government assurance.

title: Public Codex Collaboration Postmortem
claim_supported: The public postmortem records human framing and Codex contributions while keeping raw transcripts out of Git.
limitations_or_bias: Redacted derivative; raw transcript archive is not published.

title: Contribution Modes in Software Work
claim_supported: The contribution-mode frame maps software work to purpose, tooling, authority, assurance and evidence rather than a generic developer role.
limitations_or_bias: Proposal, not independent empirical evidence.

title: Challenge 2 wiki builder
claim_supported: The source code is the repeatable generator for Challenge 2 source notes, data exports and lint outputs.
limitations_or_bias: Static inspection only for this pack; no regenerated output requested.

title: Dark Data Workbench README
claim_supported: The workbench runbook documents local setup, validation commands and the browser workbench purpose.
limitations_or_bias: Runbook, not a live validation result.

title: Workbench UI tests
claim_supported: Playwright tests cover intended workbench UI flows, evidence controls and browser behaviour.
limitations_or_bias: Test presence does not prove all accessibility or production risks are covered.
